semantic_matched_intents.quote: You write clean, production-ready code with no over-engineering.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already does not add features, refactors, or abstractions beyond what the task requires by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: You think before you write: read first, understand the existing structure, then act.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already plans approach before implementation on non-trivial work by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: You are direct, concise, and you never waste Ali's time with obvious explanations or trailing summaries.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already provides short, direct answers, avoids obvious explanations, and does not provide trailing end-of-turn summaries by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Use clear, descriptive commit messages
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already focuses commit messages on WHY not WHAT, uses 1–2 sentences, and imperative-mood summary by default, which results in clear, descriptive messages.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: User confirmation prompts for destructive operations
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already asks before destructive operations by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Break down into specific implementation steps
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already decomposes non-trivial tasks by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Update file with implementation progress and activity summaries
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already tracks progress (TaskCreate) by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Mark task as "Completed" in todo.md
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already marks tasks complete as soon as done by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: 3. `_todo/pending/` for active tasks requiring progress updates
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already tracks progress (TaskCreate) by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Comments should prioritize explaining the "why" over the "what."
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already writes why-not-what comments by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Focus on the purpose behind non-obvious code, business logic, or complex algorithmic choices. This is the most important role of comments.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already writes why-not-what comments by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: DO NOT write comments for simple, self-explanatory code.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already avoids writing comments for simple, self-explanatory code by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: For complex tasks, propose a step-by-step plan before coding.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already plans its approach before implementation on non-trivial work by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Keep commits small and focused.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already creates new commits rather than amending, which implies focused commits by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Avoid comments that restate the code - they become stale
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already does not explain WHAT the code does.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Complex algorithms or non-obvious "gotchas"
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already only writes a comment when the WHY is non-obvious.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Avoid obvious branching comments - `if (x)` rarely needs `// when x is true`
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already does not explain WHAT the code does.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Title: imperative mood, lowercase after colon, no period
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already uses imperative-mood summary for commit messages.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Keep bodies concise (1-2 lines typical, trivial changes need only a title)
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already writes 1-2 sentence commit messages.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: The "why": motivation, design choices, preparation for future work
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already focuses commit messages on WHY.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: comments explain why, not what
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already writes why-not-what comments by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: build only what's needed now. No speculative features or "future extensibility".
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already does not add features, refactors, or abstractions beyond what the task requires by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Write concise commit messages explaining *why*, not *what*.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already writes why-not-what commit messages by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Separate logical changes into distinct commits.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already decomposes non-trivial tasks and creates new commits for distinct changes by default.
